name: Debate (Strict)
description: Like Debate, but the moderator must produce a parseable verdict (pass/blockers + confidence + escalate).

phases.name: Frame
phases.prompt: State the question or decision to debate. Provide context.

phases.name: Opening Arguments
phases.prompt: Make your opening case. Be specific and evidence-based. Cite [file:line] when claiming about code.

phases.name: Rebuttals
phases.prompt: Respond directly to the opposing argument. Where are they wrong? Provide your own independent analysis.

phases.name: Verdict
phases.prompt:
Deliver a structured verdict. Required output format (each on its own line):

verdict: <name of for-side option> OR <name of against-side option>
confidence: <0-10>
escalate: true | false
consensus_class: true_consensus | partial_consensus | false_consensus

Reasoning (3-5 sentences max) citing specific statements from for/against.

Hard rules:
- Forbidden verdicts: 'both have merit', 'depends on context', any hedge.
- If you cannot decide cleanly, set escalate: true. Do not invent a middle answer.
- Quote concrete claims from each side as evidence.
